description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST enforce: BOLA at ORDER 1 (gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId), SETNX(gig-accept-lock:{gigId}) at ORDER 2, OCC bid status check (bid.status === OPEN) at ORDER 3, storeDocument(audit) before GigAccepted (DNA-8), GigAcceptanceFailed on all guard failures.
blockConditions: T613 does not check BOLA at ORDER 1 before SETNX
T613 acquires SETNX lock before BOLA ownership check
T613 does not check bid.status === OPEN before emitting GigAccepted
T613 calls enqueue(GigAccepted) before storeDocument(audit)
T613 returns DataProcessResult.failure without emitting GigAcceptanceFailed
passConditions: T613 validates gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId at ORDER 1
T613 acquires SETNX lock on 'gig-accept-lock:{gigId}' at ORDER 2
T613 validates bid.status === OPEN at ORDER 3 after lock acquisition
T613 storeDocument(audit) before enqueue(GigAccepted) — DNA-8
GigAcceptanceFailed emitted on BOLA violation, duplicate lock, and stale bid

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST prevent double-acceptance via SETNX lock. Concurrent accept requests for the same gigId MUST be blocked — only one freelancer can be accepted per gig.
blockConditions: T613 allows two concurrent accept requests to both succeed for the same gigId
T613 does not check for existing acceptance lock before proceeding
xiigen-gig-accept-locks has two records for the same gigId after concurrent requests
passConditions: T613 acquires SETNX lock at ORDER 2 before any write
Second concurrent accept for same gigId returns alreadyAccepted:true
Only one GigAccepted event emitted per gigId regardless of concurrent requests

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST validate that the accepting client actually owns the gig posting (BOLA). A freelancer or other tenant must not be able to accept a gig on behalf of a client.
blockConditions: T613 allows a non-owner tenant to accept a gig posting
T613 accepts a bid without verifying gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId
T613 trusts clientTenantId from request payload instead of ALS
passConditions: T613 fetches gigPosting from database and compares clientTenantId to ALS.tenantId
Mismatch between gigPosting.clientTenantId and ALS.tenantId → BOLA_VIOLATION rejection
GigAcceptanceFailed emitted with reason BOLA_VIOLATION on ownership mismatch

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST validate bid freshness via OCC check after SETNX lock. A withdrawn bid must not be accepted even if it passed the earlier BOLA and SETNX guards.
blockConditions: T613 accepts a bid without checking bid.status after acquiring the lock
T613 checks bid status before SETNX lock — TOCTOU race window exists
T613 trusts bidStatus from event payload instead of database lookup
passConditions: T613 queries bid status from database at ORDER 3, after SETNX lock acquired
bid.status !== OPEN → GigAcceptanceFailed with BID_NOT_OPEN
OCC check uses current database state — not cached or payload-supplied status

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST emit both GigAccepted (success) and GigAcceptanceFailed (failure). Missing either event leaves downstream consumers without lifecycle signals.
blockConditions: T613 success path does not emit GigAccepted event
T613 failure path does not emit GigAcceptanceFailed before returning
T613 emits GigAccepted before storeDocument(audit) — DNA-8 violation
passConditions: GigAccepted emitted with gigId, bidId, freelancerId, clientTenantId, acceptedAt
GigAcceptanceFailed emitted with gigId, bidId, reason on all guard failures
GigAccepted only emitted after storeDocument(audit) succeeds

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST use IGigPostingService (F1537) for BOLA check, IBidStatusService (F1538) for OCC bid status, IGigAcceptAuditService (F1539) for audit trail. Direct database access without fabric interfaces violates Rule 1.
blockConditions: T613 imports database SDK directly instead of using fabric interfaces
T613 performs BOLA check without F1537 IGigPostingService
T613 performs OCC bid check without F1538 IBidStatusService
passConditions: T613 injects F1537 IGigPostingService for gig posting lookup and BOLA check
T613 injects F1538 IBidStatusService for OCC bid status validation
T613 injects F1539 IGigAcceptAuditService for acceptance audit trail

description: T613 generation prompt MUST include: BOLA_OWNERSHIP_CHECK machine component, SETNX_GIG_ACCEPT_LOCK machine component, OCC_BID_STATUS_GUARD machine component, DNA-8 audit before GigAccepted. Three guards in correct ORDER 1-2-3 sequence.
blockConditions: Generation prompt omits BOLA check directive
Generation prompt specifies SETNX before BOLA
Generation prompt does not specify OCC bid status check after SETNX
passConditions: Generation prompt includes BOLA_OWNERSHIP_CHECK at ORDER 1 directive
Generation prompt includes SETNX_GIG_ACCEPT_LOCK at ORDER 2 directive
Generation prompt includes OCC_BID_STATUS_GUARD at ORDER 3 directive
Generation prompt specifies DNA-8 storeDocument(audit) before GigAccepted

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST enforce: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS as compile-time constant at ORDER 1, sum validation at ORDER 2, OCC write at ORDER 3, storeDocument(audit) before FreelancerContractActivated (DNA-8), ContractFieldImmutable and MilestoneSumMismatch on failures.
blockConditions: T614 reads CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS from database or FREEDOM config at runtime
T614 allows write attempt to proceed before checking immutable fields
T614 uses plain storeDocument instead of storeDocumentWithOCC
T614 skips sum validation when milestones array is provided
T614 emits FreelancerContractActivated before storeDocument(audit)
passConditions: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS defined as compile-time const at module level
T614 checks immutable fields at ORDER 1 before any database access
T614 validates milestones.reduce(sum) === contractTotal at ORDER 2
T614 uses storeDocumentWithOCC with versionPin at ORDER 3
T614 storeDocument(audit) before FreelancerContractActivated enqueue — DNA-8

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST enforce that milestones exactly sum to contractTotal. Any escrow gap (milestones sum < contractTotal) or over-allocation (sum > total) must be rejected.
blockConditions: T614 allows milestones sum that is less than contractTotal — escrow funding gap
T614 allows milestones sum that exceeds contractTotal — over-allocation
T614 performs sum validation after writing the contract record
passConditions: T614 validates milestones.reduce(sum) === contractTotal before any write
MilestoneSumMismatch emitted with expected and actual values on failure
Sum validation uses number equality — no floating point rounding tolerance

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST protect contract core fields (clientId, contractTotal, gigId, freelancerId) from modification after activation. These fields form the legal contract basis.
blockConditions: T614 allows clientId to be updated after contract activation
T614 allows contractTotal to be changed after contract activation
T614 allows gigId or freelancerId to be swapped after activation
passConditions: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS includes clientId, contractTotal, gigId, freelancerId
Any write attempt to an immutable field returns FIELD_IMMUTABLE immediately
ContractFieldImmutable emitted with the rejected field name on every rejection

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST define CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS as compile-time constant. A runtime-configurable immutable field list is attackable — an operator could remove contractTotal from the list and allow contract amount manipulation.
blockConditions: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS loaded from FREEDOM config or database at runtime
CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS can be modified via any API or configuration endpoint
A superadmin bypass path exists that skips immutable field enforcement
passConditions: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS defined as TypeScript const at module level — immutable at runtime
No runtime path can modify CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS
Immutable field enforcement applies to all callers including superadmin

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST emit FreelancerContractActivated (success), ContractFieldImmutable (immutable field), MilestoneSumMismatch (sum error), ContractUpdateConflict (OCC conflict). All 4 event types required for downstream completeness.
blockConditions: T614 success path does not emit FreelancerContractActivated
T614 immutable field rejection does not emit ContractFieldImmutable
T614 sum mismatch does not emit MilestoneSumMismatch
passConditions: FreelancerContractActivated emitted after successful update with milestoneCount and contractTotal
ContractFieldImmutable emitted with field name on immutable field rejection
MilestoneSumMismatch emitted with expected and actual on sum failure
ContractUpdateConflict emitted on OCC conflict — no FreelancerContractActivated on conflict

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST use IContractMilestoneService (F1540) for OCC writes and IContractAuditService (F1541) for audit. OCC capability comes from F1540 — plain database SDK bypasses OCC enforcement.
blockConditions: T614 does not inject IContractMilestoneService (F1540) for contract writes
T614 implements OCC manually without using F1540 fabric interface
T614 writes audit without using F1541 IContractAuditService
passConditions: T614 injects F1540 IContractMilestoneService for all contract read/write with OCC
T614 injects F1541 IContractAuditService for contract audit trail
All contract writes routed through F1540 — no direct database access

description: T614 generation prompt MUST include: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS_GUARD compile-time constant, MILESTONE_SUM_VALIDATION at ORDER 2, OCC_CONTRACT_WRITE at ORDER 3, DNA-8 audit before FreelancerContractActivated.
blockConditions: Generation prompt specifies CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS as runtime-configurable
Generation prompt omits sum validation directive
Generation prompt does not specify OCC write requirement
passConditions: Generation prompt includes CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS_GUARD — const at module scope
Generation prompt includes MILESTONE_SUM_VALIDATION at ORDER 2
Generation prompt includes OCC_CONTRACT_WRITE at ORDER 3
Generation prompt specifies DNA-8 audit before FreelancerContractActivated

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST enforce: delivery gate validation before funds release, release path uses storeDocument(RELEASED) never deleteDocument, dispute path uses updateDocument(DISPUTED) only, LIFO [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] compensation, storeDocument(audit) before every event (DNA-8).
blockConditions: T615 releases funds without checking delivery.status === SUBMITTED
T615 calls deleteDocument on milestone during dispute or release
T615 moves funds during the dispute path
T615 uses FIFO compensation order instead of LIFO
T615 emits MilestoneReleased before storeDocument(audit)
passConditions: T615 validates delivery.status === SUBMITTED at ORDER 1 before any funds movement
T615 release path: storeDocument(milestone, status:RELEASED) — no deleteDocument
T615 dispute path: updateDocument(status:DISPUTED) only — no funds movement
T615 LIFO: [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] registered forward, executed reversed
T615 storeDocument(audit) before MilestoneReleased and MilestoneDisputed — DNA-8

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST prevent escrow release without a submitted delivery. Client cannot release funds for work that has not been delivered.
blockConditions: T615 allows funds release when no delivery with status SUBMITTED exists for the milestone
T615 treats DRAFT deliveries as submitted for release purposes
T615 bypasses delivery gate when release is requested by an operator
passConditions: T615 queries deliveries with milestoneId and status SUBMITTED before release
DELIVERY_NOT_SUBMITTED returned when no qualifying delivery found
Delivery gate validation cannot be bypassed by any caller

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST read milestone_auto_release_days from FREEDOM config for timer gate-opener. Hardcoded auto-release duration prevents operators from adjusting dispute windows per their business model.
blockConditions: T615 uses hardcoded auto-release duration (e.g. 14) instead of FREEDOM config
T615 does not support timer gate-opener for auto-release
T615 reads auto-release duration from environment variable instead of FREEDOM config
passConditions: T615 reads milestone_auto_release_days from FREEDOM config via database fabric
Timer gate-opener duration is operator-configurable via milestone_auto_release_days
Default fallback applied only when FREEDOM config key is absent

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST use LIFO compensation to prevent incomplete reversal states. FIFO compensation can leave the system in a state where the gig is available for re-acceptance but funds are still in transit.
blockConditions: T615 LIFO chain executes REFUND_MILESTONE before RESTORE_GIG_STATUS (FIFO order)
T615 compensates all prior milestones when one fails — cascading incorrect reversal
T615 does not use .reverse() to execute compensation chain in LIFO order
passConditions: T615 compensation chain registered forward [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS]
T615 executor reverses chain: [...ESCROW_COMPENSATION_CHAIN].reverse() before execution
First compensation step executed: RESTORE_GIG_STATUS — blocks re-acceptance during refund

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST emit MilestoneReleased (release path), MilestoneDisputed (dispute path), EscrowCompensationStarted (compensation path). All 3 event types required for downstream completeness.
blockConditions: T615 release path does not emit MilestoneReleased
T615 dispute path does not emit MilestoneDisputed
T615 compensation start does not emit EscrowCompensationStarted with chain
passConditions: MilestoneReleased emitted with milestoneId, contractId, releasedAt, deliveryId after release
MilestoneDisputed emitted with milestoneId, contractId, disputeReason after dispute
EscrowCompensationStarted emitted with LIFO chain [RESTORE_GIG_STATUS, REFUND_MILESTONE] on failure

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST use IDeliveryGateService (F1542) for delivery validation, IEscrowMilestoneService (F1543) for milestone state transitions, IEscrowAuditService (F1544) for audit trail.
blockConditions: T615 does not inject IDeliveryGateService (F1542) for delivery validation
T615 implements delivery check without using F1542 fabric interface
T615 performs direct database access for milestone status transitions
passConditions: T615 injects F1542 IDeliveryGateService for delivery status validation
T615 injects F1543 IEscrowMilestoneService for milestone release/dispute transitions
T615 injects F1544 IEscrowAuditService for escrow audit trail — PLATFORM_ONLY

description: T615 generation prompt MUST include: DELIVERY_GATE_VALIDATION machine component, LIFO_MILESTONE_COMPENSATION with [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] order, no deleteDocument directive, DNA-8 audit before events.
blockConditions: Generation prompt specifies delivery gate as optional
Generation prompt uses FIFO compensation order
Generation prompt does not specify no-deleteDocument constraint
passConditions: Generation prompt includes DELIVERY_GATE_VALIDATION — delivery.status === SUBMITTED required
Generation prompt specifies LIFO chain [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] forward, executed reversed
Generation prompt specifies no deleteDocument on any escrow path
Generation prompt specifies DNA-8 audit before MilestoneReleased and MilestoneDisputed

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST enforce: direction check at ORDER 1 (VALID_REVIEW_DIRECTIONS compile-time), duplicate direction check at ORDER 2, append-only write at ORDER 3 (no updateDocument), comment excluded from audit at ORDER 4, FreelancerReviewSubmitted at ORDER 5 only after audit.
blockConditions: T616 allows an invalid review direction
T616 allows two reviews in the same direction for the same engagement
T616 calls updateDocument on a review record after submission
T616 includes comment in audit record
T616 emits FreelancerReviewSubmitted before storeDocument(audit)
passConditions: T616 validates direction against VALID_REVIEW_DIRECTIONS compile-time constant at ORDER 1
T616 queries for existing review in same direction at ORDER 2 and rejects duplicates
T616 writes review via storeDocument only — no updateDocument on review records
T616 audit record excludes comment field — no PII in platform audit
T616 storeDocument(audit) before FreelancerReviewSubmitted enqueue — DNA-8

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST enforce single-direction review constraint. One review per direction per engagement is the domain invariant — both CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER and FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT are valid but each may only be submitted once.
blockConditions: T616 allows a client to submit two CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER reviews for the same engagement
T616 allows a freelancer to submit two FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT reviews for the same engagement
T616 does not check for existing review in same direction before writing
passConditions: T616 queries xiigen-freelancer-reviews for existing review with engagementId and direction
DuplicateReviewRejected emitted when duplicate direction found
Each direction can be used exactly once per engagement — subsequent attempts rejected

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST exclude comment from audit record. Platform operators who export audit data must not see free-text review comments which may contain PII, personal disputes, or defamatory content.
blockConditions: T616 audit record includes comment field with PLATFORM_ONLY knowledgeScope
T616 includes comment in the FreelancerReviewSubmitted event payload
T616 does not distinguish between review record storage and audit storage for comment handling
passConditions: T616 audit record stored without comment field — only reviewId, direction, rating, reviewerId, targetId
Comment stored in review record with PRIVATE knowledgeScope only
FreelancerReviewSubmitted event does not include comment field

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST be append-only. No updateDocument on review records after submission. A mutable review system enables reputation manipulation — reviewers can change their rating after receiving payment for revision.
blockConditions: T616 provides an update endpoint that calls updateDocument on review records
T616 allows rating to be changed after initial submission
T616 treats review update as a create with upsert — effectively allowing modification
passConditions: T616 uses storeDocument only for review writes — no updateDocument in service code
Review records have immutable fields after storeDocument — no update path exists
Reviewer cannot change rating or direction after initial submission

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST emit FreelancerReviewSubmitted (success) and DuplicateReviewRejected (duplicate direction). Both events required for downstream reputation signal processing.
blockConditions: T616 success path does not emit FreelancerReviewSubmitted
T616 duplicate rejection does not emit DuplicateReviewRejected
T616 emits FreelancerReviewSubmitted before storeDocument(audit) — DNA-8 violation
passConditions: FreelancerReviewSubmitted emitted with reviewId, direction, rating, reviewerId, targetId — no comment
DuplicateReviewRejected emitted with engagementId, direction, reason on duplicate
FreelancerReviewSubmitted only emitted after storeDocument(audit) succeeds

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST use IReviewStoreService (F1545) for review write and duplicate check, IReviewAuditService (F1546) for comment-excluded audit trail.
blockConditions: T616 does not inject IReviewStoreService (F1545) for review storage
T616 implements duplicate direction check without using F1545
T616 writes audit without using F1546 IReviewAuditService
passConditions: T616 injects F1545 IReviewStoreService for review storage and duplicate direction check
T616 injects F1546 IReviewAuditService for comment-excluded audit trail
All review writes and duplicate checks routed through F1545 — no direct database access

description: T616 generation prompt MUST include: VALID_REVIEW_DIRECTIONS_GUARD compile-time constant, SINGLE_DIRECTION_DUPLICATE_CHECK at ORDER 2, APPEND_ONLY_REVIEW_WRITE (no updateDocument), COMMENT_EXCLUDED_AUDIT directive.
blockConditions: Generation prompt specifies review directions as runtime-configurable
Generation prompt omits duplicate direction check directive

passConditions: Generation prompt includes VALID_REVIEW_DIRECTIONS_GUARD — compile-time const

Generation prompt includes COMMENT_EXCLUDED_AUDIT — comment absent from audit record

description: T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway scope isolation (FC-32): tenantId exclusively from ALS. gigPosting.clientTenantId compared to ALS.tenantId — not from request body. Gig postings are PRIVATE per tenant. Acceptance locks are PRIVATE per tenant. Audit records are PRIVATE per tenant.
blockConditions: T613 accepts tenantId from event payload or request body for BOLA comparison
T613 stores gig accept lock with GLOBAL or incorrect knowledgeScope
T613 exposes audit records from other tenants
passConditions: T613 resolves tenantId via this.cls.get(TENANT_CONTEXT_KEY)?.tenantId — ALS only

description: T614 MilestoneContractManager scope isolation (FC-32): tenantId exclusively from ALS. Contract records are PRIVATE per tenant. Contract audit records are PRIVATE per tenant. Request body tenantId ignored if present.
blockConditions: T614 accepts tenantId from event payload or request body
T614 stores contract with GLOBAL knowledgeScope
T614 allows cross-tenant contract reads
passConditions: T614 resolves tenantId via this.cls.get(TENANT_CONTEXT_KEY)?.tenantId — ALS only
All contract storeDocument calls include knowledgeScope: PRIVATE
Contract queries filter by tenantId from ALS — no cross-tenant access

description: T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController scope isolation (FC-32): tenantId exclusively from ALS. Milestone and delivery records are PRIVATE per tenant. Escrow audit records are PRIVATE per tenant. No cross-tenant milestone access.
blockConditions: T615 accepts tenantId from event payload for milestone lookup
T615 stores milestone or delivery with GLOBAL knowledgeScope
T615 allows cross-tenant delivery validation
passConditions: T615 resolves tenantId via this.cls.get(TENANT_CONTEXT_KEY)?.tenantId — ALS only
All milestone storeDocument calls include knowledgeScope: PRIVATE
Delivery gate queries filter by milestoneId owned by ALS tenant

description: T616 FreelancerReviewWriter scope isolation (FC-32): tenantId exclusively from ALS. Review records stored with PRIVATE knowledgeScope. Audit records stored without comment to prevent PII in PLATFORM_ONLY scope. No cross-tenant review reads.
blockConditions: T616 accepts tenantId from event payload or request body
